title: [Kits] memory field not working in mixin kits
body:
Using the "memory" field in a mixin kit has no effect - the content is not appended to aiFilename.

```yaml
schemaVersion: "1"
kind: mixin
name: test-memory

memory: |
  Pineapple
```

--> add to a claude sandbox and grep the CLAUDE.md file in the project parent directory for Pineapple 

["memory"](https://docs.docker.com/ai/sandboxes/customize/kits/#memory) is a top-level field (although the spec reference lists it scoped to the agent block) and I would have expected its content to replace or be appended to the CLAUDE.md file as I am extending the [build-in claude agent kit](https://docs.docker.com/ai/sandboxes/customize/kits/#example-the-built-in-claude-agent). 

Maybe this is expected behavior and only the docs need clarification. 

But having this work in some way to apply "global" CLAUDE.md content through a kit would be great - although standard merge rules would mean the field is getting replaced, which doesn't work well when you can stack kits. The default sbx-specific memory that should always be present could possibly be moved in to an agent.memory field, but for stacking kits it would likely require to use concatenation instead of replacement.

title: Extend Conversion Tracking for Event Providers with two new custom dimensions
body:
## Feature Description

Building on the existing conversion event tracking infrastructure introduced in the Initial Conversion Event Infrastructure epic, this issue extends Site Kit's event providers (`assets/js/event-providers`) to send two new custom dimensions alongside the conversion events they already track:

1. `googlesitekit_event_provider` — identifies the plugin source of a tracked event (e.g. WooCommerce, Contact Form 7). This enables the Source Tabs in the Online Store Performance widget and the Info Icons on Form tabs in the Lead Generation Performance widget.
2. `googlesitekit_form_id` — the ID of the form that triggered a lead generation event. Following the existing pattern of `googlesitekit_post_author` and `googlesitekit_post_category`, only the form ID is tracked (not the title), as the title can be queried from `wp_posts` at reporting time. See [Conversion Providers Form ID extraction](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1y9V3wKap3QSrUHPMI58qO_Q0CwW0xIY6-rSOZkbG4xg/edit?gid=0#gid=0) for the spike findings on form ID extraction per provider.

Both new custom dimensions must also be added to the existing custom dimension definitions and valid-slug allowlists in JS and PHP so existing routes accept and track them correctly.

<!-- Do not alter or remove anything below. The following sections will be managed by moderators only. -->

## Acceptance criteria

- When a supported event provider plugin fires a conversion event tracked by Site Kit (within `assets/js/event-providers`), the `googlesitekit_event_provider` custom dimension should be included in the event payload, with a value identifying the specific plugin source (e.g. `"woocommerce"`, `"contact-form-7"`).
- When a supported lead generation event provider fires a form completion event (i.e. `submit_lead_form`, `contact`, or `generate_lead`), the `googlesitekit_form_id` custom dimension should also be included in the event payload, containing the integer or string ID of the form that was submitted.
- `googlesitekit_form_id` should **not** be sent for e-commerce events (`purchase`, `add_to_cart`).
- Both `googlesitekit_event_provider` and `googlesitekit_form_id` should be added to the custom dimension definitions and valid-slug allowlists in both JS and PHP, so that the existing custom dimension creation and sync routes accept them.
- The implementation should follow the existing patterns used by `googlesitekit_post_author` and `googlesitekit_post_category` for tracking ID-based custom dimensions.


## Implementation Brief

* [ ] <!-- One or more bullet points for how to technically implement the feature. Make sure to include changes to Storybook and visual regression tests where relevant. -->

### Test Coverage

* <!-- One or more bullet points for how to implement automated tests to verify the feature works. -->

## QA Brief

* <!-- One or more bullet points for how to test that the feature works as expected. -->

## Changelog entry

* <!-- One sentence summarizing the PR, to be used in the changelog. -->

## Feature Description

Building on the existing conversion event tracking infrastructure introduced in the Initial Conversion Event Infrastructure epic, this issue extends Site Kit's event providers

title: Don't use `system-ui`, strip it from `--ifm-font-family-base`
body: ### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues). ### Motivation `system-ui` is mainly designed for well i18ned web-based desktop apps (e.g. Electron-based). It is not designed for the font for documentation sites. e.g.: 1. `system-ui` in a Chinese page lοοκѕ lικе τһιѕ in Japanese Windows devices and vice versa. The shape of some characters are not intended. 2. https://docusaurus.io/zh-CN/ Windows + Japanese locale ❌️ (system-ui) The 1st character does not have an ideal shape as a standard Chinese printing font. The 2nd character is incorrect. It's Japanese unofficial abbreviation form. ✅️ (modify CSS via F12 to remove system-ui) (modify CSS via F12 to use the font used as system-ui in Chinese Windows) https://docusaurus-i18n-staging.netlify.app/ja/docs/migration/v3 ❌️ (system-ui in Japanese Windows) Too narrow. This font is helpful only in UI elements whose widths are limited. This font is designed to prevent the width of UI elements intended for English text from expanding excessively when Japanese text is inserted. It is not suitable for normal use outside of PowerPoint. ❌️💀❌️ (replace system-ui with a default UI font in Chinese Windows to simulate Chinese locale) Looks very ugly as Japanese text. Some character have incorrect shape (e.g. 非, 直 → 非, 直 ) ✅️ (remove system-ui) The shape of Japanese (and Chinese) character for the main text should be square like this. This should be fixed by v4 release. https://infinnie.github.io/blog/2017/systemui.html https://heistak.github.io/your-code-displays-japanese-wrong/ https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/3658 github.com/mdn/content/issues/41244 https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/font-family#system-ui ### Self-service - [ ] I'd be willing to do some initial work on this proposal myself.

title: Rewind support
body:
Durable Functions and the Azure Storage backend both support orchestration rewind, which allows restoring an orchestration instance back to its previous state after it has failed. This functionality should be made available to this SDK.

Rewind is currently in preview status. As part of this work, we should bring it onto the path to GA. This would likely require some changes to DTFx, such as completing the following work item: https://github.com/Azure/durabletask/issues/731.

### Checklist

- [ ] Core DTFx updates
- [ ] New DF API surface area
- [ ] gRPC/Protobuf contract support
- [ ] Integration tests

title: Conditional branching broken on Argo Workflows v3.7.11+ / v4.0.2+
body:
## Description

Flows using conditional branching (`self.next({...}, condition=...)`) hang indefinitely on Argo Workflows v3.7.11+ and v4.0.2+. The convergence step after conditional branches is never scheduled.

## Reproducer

The [metaflow-music-agent](https://github.com/outerbounds/metaflow-music-agent) example reproduces this. The `ResearchAgentFlow` uses:

```python
self.next(
    {
        "continue": self.research,
        "done": self.done,
        "unknown": self.unknown,
        "failed": self.failed,
    },
    condition="decision",
)
```

`done`, `unknown`, and `failed` all converge on `agent_complete` -> `join` -> `end`. Only one branch executes per foreach iteration; the others are skipped.

Run with:
```bash
python agentflow.py --environment=pypi --with kubernetes run --max-artists 2
```

The research steps complete successfully, each branch reaches `done` or `unknown`, but `agent_complete` is never scheduled and the workflow hangs in `Running` state.

## Root Cause

Two interacting issues:

### 1. How Metaflow generates `input-paths` for conditional join steps

For a convergence step like `agent_complete`, the generated Argo DAG template references outputs from **all** conditional branches unconditionally:

```yaml
arguments:
  parameters:
    - name: input-paths
      value: "argo-{{workflow.name}}/:done/{{tasks.done.outputs.parameters.task-id}},failed/{{tasks.failed.outputs.parameters.task-id}},unknown/{{tasks.unknown.outputs.parameters.task-id}}"
depends: "done.Succeeded || failed.Succeeded || unknown.Succeeded"
```

The `depends` clause is correct (OR), but `input-paths` references `task-id` from all three branches. Skipped branches produce no outputs.

Metaflow's intended workaround (introduced in #2560) is to base64-encode the value and filter out unresolved paths at runtime via `conditional_input_paths.py`. This depends on Argo passing through unresolved `{{tasks.X...}}` expressions as literal strings.

### 2. Argo Workflows changed parameter resolution behavior

Argo PR [argoproj/argo-workflows#15442](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/pull/15442) ("fix: requeue workflow if expected variables are missing", Feb 26 2026) changed `dag.go` from:

```go
// Before (v3.7.10, v4.0.1 and earlier) — unresolved expressions passed through
newTaskStr, err := template.Replace(ctx, string(taskBytes), mergedParams, true)
```

to:

```go
// After (v3.7.11, v4.0.2+) — unresolved tasks.*/steps.* references cause requeue
newTaskStr, err := template.ReplaceStrict(ctx, string(taskBytes), mergedParams, []string{"tasks", "steps"})
if err != nil {
    if template.IsMissingVariableErr(err) {
        woc.requeue()
        return nil, ErrRequeue  // pod never created, runtime filter never runs
    }
}
```

The controller now requeues indefinitely when it can't resolve `{{tasks.failed.outputs.parameters.task-id}}` for a skipped task. The `conditional_input_paths.py` runtime filter never gets a chance to execute.

### Controller logs confirm this

```
level=WARN msg="was unable to find variable" error="failed to resolve {{tasks.failed.outputs.parameters.task-id}}"
level=WARN msg="was unable to find variable" error="failed to resolve {{tasks.done.outputs.parameters.task-id}}"
level=WARN msg="was unable to obtain the node" taskName=agent-complete
```

## Affected Versions

| Argo Version | Behavior | Conditionals work? |
|---|---|---|
| v3.5.x - v3.7.10 | `Replace(allowUnresolved=true)` | Yes |
| **v3.7.11+** | `ReplaceStrict` | **No** |
| v4.0.0 - v4.0.1 | `Replace(allowUnresolved=true)` | Yes |
| **v4.0.2+** | `ReplaceStrict` | **No** |

Tested with Metaflow 2.19.22. Likely affects all versions since #2560 (Aug 2025).

## History

For context, the original conditionals PR (#2550, Aug 19 2025) used safe Argo expressions that handled skipped tasks correctly:

```
{{=(get(tasks['done']?.outputs?.parameters, 'task-id') ?? 'no-task')}}
```

This was removed two days later in #2560 (Aug 21 2025) because `?.` optional chaining doesn't work inside foreach (`withParam` nested DAGs evaluate all `?.` as false). The replacement strategy relied on Argo's passthrough behavior, which is no longer guaranteed.

## Argo-side tracking

This is related to [argoproj/argo-workflows#15737](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/issues/15737). A fix (PR [#15736](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/pull/15736)) has been merged and cherry-picked to v4.0 and v3.7, but that fix targets expanded task properties (`withItems`/`withParams`). It may not fully cover the skipped conditional branch case — worth verifying once v3.7.12 / v4.0.4 are released.

## Workaround

Downgrade Argo Workflows to v3.7.10 or v4.0.1.

## Environment

- Metaflow: 2.19.22
- Argo Workflows: tested on v4.0.3 and v3.7.11
- Kubernetes: EKS
- Python: 3.12

title: GPT-5.5 performance and reliability seem significantly worse today
body:
Today, GPT-5.5 has felt significantly worse than usual in Codex.

It went through multiple rounds of conversation but still failed to fix a bug. Even worse, some of the attempted changes ended up breaking parts of my project that were previously working.

Over the past two days, this noticeably degraded Codex behavior has also caused repeated failed bug-fix attempts followed by rollbacks, wasting a large amount of my usage. My $200 Pro plan quota has already consumed around 60% of the weekly limit in just these two days because of these unsuccessful cycles.

This does not feel like normal model behavior compared with previous sessions. The debugging quality, consistency, and ability to preserve existing functionality all seem noticeably degraded today.

Is anyone else seeing the same issue with GPT-5.5/Codex today?

title: `result_large_err` doesn't trigger for async fn that returns a Result
body:
### Summary

`result_large_err` doesn't trigger for async fn that returns a Result

### Lint Name

result_large_err

### Reproducer

I tried this code:

```rust
pub enum MyError {
    A([u8; 256]),
}

pub async fn test() -> Result<(), MyError> {
    Ok(())
}
```

I expected to see this happen like the non-async fn:
```
warning: the `Err`-variant returned from this function is very large
  --> wgpu/src/backend/mod.rs:20:18
   |
16 |     A([u8;256]),
   |     ----------- the largest variant contains at least 256 bytes
...
20 | pub fn test() -> Result<(),MyError> {
   |                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |
   = help: try reducing the size of `backend::MyError`, for example by boxing large elements or replacing it with `Box<backend::MyError>`
   = help: for further information visit https://rust-lang.github.io/rust-clippy/rust-1.93.0/index.html#result_large_err
   = note: `#[warn(clippy::result_large_err)]` on by default
```

Instead, No warnings happened.


### Version

```text
rustc 1.93.1 (01f6ddf75 2026-02-11)
binary: rustc
commit-hash: 01f6ddf7588f42ae2d7eb0a2f21d44e8e96674cf
commit-date: 2026-02-11
host: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
release: 1.93.1
LLVM version: 21.1.8
```

<!-- TRIAGEBOT_START -->

<!-- TRIAGEBOT_ASSIGN_START -->

<!-- TRIAGEBOT_ASSIGN_END -->
<!-- TRIAGEBOT_END -->

title: [BUG] UnknownFormatConversionException in StaticResolvableString when TextFieldController.contentDescription wraps raw user input
body:
## Summary

`StaticResolvableString.resolve()` runs `String.format(value, ...)` where `value` is the user-typed text field value, because several `TextFieldController` implementations build their accessibility `contentDescription` directly from the field value via the `String.resolvableString` extension:

```kotlin
// payments-ui-core/.../CvcController.kt
override val contentDescription: StateFlow<ResolvableString> = _fieldValue.mapAsStateFlow {
    it.asIndividualDigits().resolvableString
}
```

```kotlin
// stripe-core/.../ResolvableStringUtils.kt
val String.resolvableString: ResolvableString
    get() = StaticResolvableString(this, emptyList())

// stripe-core/.../StaticResolvableString.kt
override fun resolve(context: Context): String {
    return value.format(*resolveArgs(context, args))
}
```

Effectively, **`String.format` is being run over raw user input as the format string**. Any `%X` sequence Java's `Formatter` doesn't recognise (e.g. `%@`, `%X` where X isn't a Java conversion, `%` at end of string, etc.) crashes the host app with `UnknownFormatConversionException` / `MissingFormatArgumentException` / `IllegalFormatConversionException`.

This is reachable through PaymentSheet - we hit it in production with a user in the Add Payment Method flow, locale en_GB, on a Samsung Galaxy S23 FE / Android 16. The exact character sequence isn't recoverable from the crash report (it isn't logged for privacy reasons), but the stack matches a field's `contentDescription` being resolved while the user was editing.

## Stack trace

```
Fatal Exception: java.util.UnknownFormatConversionException: Conversion = '@'
    at java.util.Formatter$FormatSpecifier.conversion (Formatter.java:3013)
    at java.util.Formatter$FormatSpecifier.<init> (Formatter.java:3051)
    at java.util.Formatter$FormatSpecifierParser.<init> (Formatter.java:2804)
    at java.util.Formatter.parse (Formatter.java:2737)
    at java.util.Formatter.format (Formatter.java:2686)
    at java.util.Formatter.format (Formatter.java:2640)
    at java.lang.String.format (String.java:4448)
    at com.stripe.android.core.strings.StaticResolvableString.resolve (StaticResolvableString.kt:14)
    at com.stripe.android.uicore.elements.TextFieldUIKt.TextField_ZkbtPhE$lambda$22$0 (TextFieldUI.kt:218)
    at androidx.compose.ui.semantics.CoreSemanticsModifierNode.applySemantics (SemanticsModifier.kt:64)
    ...
```

Line 218 of `TextFieldUI.kt`:

```kotlin
.semantics {
    this.contentDescription = contentDescription.resolve(context)
    ...
}
```

…where `contentDescription` is collected from `textFieldController.contentDescription` and, for several controllers, was built via `String.resolvableString` from the field value.

## Code to reproduce

A unit test against any `TextFieldController` whose `contentDescription` is sourced from `_fieldValue.mapAsStateFlow { it.something.resolvableString }` would crash with a field value of `"%@"` (and most other `%X` sequences):

```kotlin
val controller = CvcController(...)
controller.onValueChange("%@")
controller.contentDescription.value.resolve(context)   // throws UnknownFormatConversionException
```

End-to-end repro in PaymentSheet:

1. Launch PaymentSheet's Add Payment Method UI.
2. Type / paste `%@` into a field whose controller builds `contentDescription` from the value (we saw this from a card-entry field; the same pattern exists in CvcController and others).
3. As soon as Compose re-applies semantics, `String.format` parses the value and crashes.

## Android version

Crashlytics sample event is on Android 16 (a recent Samsung device). The bug is not OS-version dependent - it's a function of `String.format` rejecting an unknown conversion specifier, which behaves the same on every JVM.

## Impacted devices

Sample event: Samsung SM-S711B (Galaxy S23 FE), Android 16, en_GB locale. Not OEM-specific; any device whose user can type or paste a `%X` sequence into an affected field is at risk.

## Installation method

Gradle dependency: `com.stripe:stripe-android:23.7.0` from Maven Central. The unsafe `String.resolvableString` use in `CvcController` (and the `StaticResolvableString` `value.format(...)` definition) is still present on `master`.

## Dependency Versions

kotlin: 2.3.0 (runtime), 2.3.10 (build)
stripe-android: 23.7.0
Android Gradle Plugin: 9.0.1
Gradle: 9.3.1

## SDK classes

- `com.stripe.android.core.strings.StaticResolvableString`
- `com.stripe.android.core.strings.ResolvableStringUtils` (the `String.resolvableString` extension)
- `com.stripe.android.uicore.elements.TextFieldUI` (line 218 in v23.7.0)
- `com.stripe.android.ui.core.elements.CvcController` (confirmed unsafe pattern)
- Likely other `TextFieldController` implementations using the same `_fieldValue.mapAsStateFlow { ….resolvableString }` pattern

## Video

No video - the crash is non-deterministic per user (depends on the character sequence in the field), but we have the full Crashlytics report and can share details privately if useful.

## Other information

The fundamental issue is treating user input as a `String.format` template. A few possible fixes, in order of preference:

1. Don't run `String.format` over a value-with-no-args. `StaticResolvableString.resolve()` could be:
   ```kotlin
   override fun resolve(context: Context): String {
       val resolved = resolveArgs(context, args)
       return if (resolved.isEmpty()) value else value.format(*resolved.toTypedArray())
   }
   ```
   This alone would fix every `String.resolvableString` call site, since they all pass `emptyList()` for args.

2. In `String.resolvableString`, escape `%` → `%%` before constructing the `StaticResolvableString`, so any later `String.format` is a no-op on the value.

3. Wrap the `value.format(...)` in `runCatching` and fall back to the unformatted `value` (most defensive, but masks real format-string bugs elsewhere).

title: pg-delta: ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE and dependent DDL must run in separate transaction scopes (55P04)
body:
## Bug report

pg-delta generates `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` and DDL that references the new enum value in the same migration transaction.

PostgreSQL rejects this with error `55P04` because newly added enum values are not visible until the transaction is committed.

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.
- [x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.

---

## Describe the bug

When a migration both:

- adds a new enum value
- and references that value in subsequent DDL

pg-delta emits all statements in the same transaction.

For example:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.user_role ADD VALUE 'store';

ALTER TABLE public.profiles
ALTER COLUMN role SET DEFAULT 'store'::public.user_role;
```

PostgreSQL rejects this because enum values added via `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` are only usable after transaction commit.

This issue affects statements such as:

- `ALTER COLUMN ... SET DEFAULT`
- `ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK (...)`

This does **not** apply to `CREATE TYPE AS ENUM (...)`, where all enum values exist at type creation time.

---

## PostgreSQL behavior

From the PostgreSQL documentation:

> New enum values must be committed before they can be used.

---

## To Reproduce

### Case 1: ADD VALUE + SET DEFAULT (55P04)

Schema in both databases:

```sql
CREATE TYPE public.user_role AS ENUM ('admin', 'user');

CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name text NOT NULL,
  role public.user_role DEFAULT 'user'
);
```

Change to target database:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.user_role ADD VALUE 'store';

ALTER TABLE public.profiles
ALTER COLUMN role SET DEFAULT 'store';
```

Generated SQL by pg-delta:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.user_role ADD VALUE 'store' AFTER 'user';

ALTER TABLE public.profiles
ALTER COLUMN role SET DEFAULT 'store'::public.user_role;
```

Apply result:

```text
ERROR: 55P04: unsafe use of new value "store" of enum type user_role
HINT: New enum values must be committed before they can be used.
```

---

### Case 2: ADD VALUE + ADD CHECK constraint (55P04)

Schema in both databases:

```sql
CREATE TYPE public.order_status AS ENUM ('pending', 'shipped');

CREATE TABLE public.orders (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  status public.order_status DEFAULT 'pending'
);
```

Change to target database:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.order_status ADD VALUE 'delivered';

ALTER TABLE public.orders ADD CONSTRAINT status_check
CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'shipped', 'delivered'));
```

Generated SQL by pg-delta:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.order_status ADD VALUE 'delivered' AFTER 'shipped';

ALTER TABLE public.orders ADD CONSTRAINT status_check
CHECK (
  status = ANY (
    ARRAY[
      'pending'::public.order_status,
      'shipped'::public.order_status,
      'delivered'::public.order_status
    ]
  )
);
```

Apply result:

```text
ERROR: 55P04: unsafe use of new value "delivered" of enum type order_status
HINT: New enum values must be committed before they can be used.
```

---

## Expected behavior

pg-delta should ideally ensure that `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` statements are executed in a separate transaction scope before any DDL that references the new enum value.

One possible approach could be emitting a transaction boundary between the enum alteration and the dependent DDL. For example:

```sql
ALTER TYPE public.user_role ADD VALUE 'store' AFTER 'user';

COMMIT;

ALTER TABLE public.profiles
ALTER COLUMN role SET DEFAULT 'store'::public.user_role;
```

---

## System information

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS
- pg-delta version: 1.0.0-alpha.25
- PostgreSQL version: 17.6
- Test framework: Roundtrip Fidelity Tests
- Browser/Client: N/A (CLI tool)

title: Security: ReDoS vulnerability in parse regex
body:
## Summary

The regex in `parse()` is vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when given long numeric input followed by an invalid unit.

## Proof of Concept

```js
const ms = require("ms");

const start = performance.now();
ms("9".repeat(1000) + "z");
console.log(performance.now() - start); // ~4.2ms per call
```

In comparison, valid input of the same length:
```js
ms("9".repeat(1000)); // ~0.001ms (matches immediately)
```

## Impact

A server parsing user-supplied time strings (cache headers, rate limit config, timeout values) can be CPU-exhausted by sending many malicious inputs. Each call takes ~4ms, so 250 concurrent requests = 1 second of blocked event loop.

**CVSS:** Low (requires user input reaching the parse function)  
**CWE:** CWE-1333 (Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity)

## Root Cause

The regex `(-?\d*\.?\d+)` has ambiguity between `\d*` and `\d+` — the engine tries all possible splits when the overall match fails.

## Suggested Fix

Add input length validation before regex execution:

```js
function parse(str) {
  str = String(str);
  if (str.length > 100) return; // no valid time string is >100 chars
  // ... existing regex logic
}
```

This is the approach we implemented in [@flupkejs/ms](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@flupkejs/ms).

## Environment

- ms version: 2.1.3 (latest)
- Node.js: v22+
- Tested: 2026-05-25

## Summary

The regex in `parse()` is vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when given long numeric input followed by an invalid unit.

## Proof of Concept

```js
const ms = require("ms");

title: support underscore variables starting with (`_`) to indicate intentionally unused variables
body:
it's common to use a single `_` to indicate an unused argument:

```py
def foo(_): # error: `foo` is missing an annotation for parameter `_` [implicit-any-parameter]
    print(_) # should be an error here instead, because it should be unused
```

title: Remove `crazy-max/ghaction-github-runtime` from reusable workflows
body:
### Context

Flux raised a supply-chain concern while evaluating `docker/github-builder`: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/5914.

The concern is fair. The reusable workflows currently use `crazy-max/ghaction-github-runtime` to expose runner-provided `ACTIONS_*` runtime variables to later steps, mainly so BuildKit can use the GitHub Actions cache backend. The action is pinned, but it is still third-party code in the trusted release path.

This weakens the goal of keeping consuming release workflows constrained to Docker-maintained workflow code plus official GitHub/Docker-owned actions.

The action logic is small enough to inline. It effectively exports existing `ACTIONS_*` environment variables into `GITHUB_ENV`.

### Proposed change

Replace the `crazy-max/ghaction-github-runtime` steps in `build.yml` and `bake.yml` with inline workflow logic owned by this repository.

Prefer writing only the required runtime variables to `$GITHUB_ENV`.

title: Context caching not working with Agent Engine (VertexAiSessionService drops cache_metadata)
body:
## 🔴 Required Information
**Describe the Bug:**
`VertexAiSessionService` does not persist `cache_metadata` or `usage_metadata` fields when writing/reading Event objects to/from Agent Engine. This causes `ContextCacheRequestProcessor` to never find prior cache metadata, so context caching creates a new cache on every invocation instead of reusing existing ones.

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Install `google-adk[eval]==1.25.1`
2. Configure an agent with `ContextCacheConfig` in App():
```text
     app = App(   
      name="my_agent",    
      root_agent=root_agent,    
      context_cache_config= ContextCacheConfig( min_tokens=2048,  ttl_seconds=1800,   cache_intervals=10),)
```
4. Run with in-memory sessions: `adk web `— send the same prompt twice in the same session. Observe `cacheMetadata` in the dev UI showing `cache_name` reuse on the second turn.
5. Run with Agent Engine sessions: `adk web --session_service_uri=agentengine://<your_engine_id> `— send the same prompt twice in the same session. Observe that every turn creates a new cache with a different fingerprint; `cache_name` is never reused.

**Expected Behavior:**
`cache_metadata` and `usage_metadata` should round-trip through Agent Engine sessions the same way they do with InMemorySessionService. The second invocation in the same session should reuse the existing cache.

**Observed Behavior:**
With `VertexAiSessionService`, every invocation creates a new cache. The `cacheMetadata` in events shows a new fingerprint and increasing `contents_count` each time, with no cache_name reuse.

**Environment Details:**
- ADK Library Version: 1.25.1
- Desktop OS: macOS
- Python Version: 3.11

**Model Information:**
 - Are you using LiteLLM: Yes/No
 - Which model is being used: gemini-2.5-pro

---

## 🟡 Optional Information

**Regression:**
N/A — this has never worked with VertexAiSessionService as far as we can tell (tested 1.19.0 and 1.25.1).

**Logs:**
With `InMemorySessionService` (working):
```text
cacheMetadata:
  cache_name: "projects/798321343024/locations/us-central1/cachedContents/6261708034305687552"
  expire_time: 1772555630.466322
  fingerprint: "e8e3e4d5226b764d"
  invocations_used: 2
  contents_count: 2
  created_at: 1772553830.466322
```

With `VertexAiSessionService` (broken — new cache every turn):
```text
cacheMetadata:
  fingerprint: "c3f575505d7ec95c"
  contents_count: 6
```

**Minimal Reproduction Code:**
```python
from google.adk.agents import Agent
from google.adk.app import App
from google.adk.context_cache import ContextCacheConfig

root_agent = Agent(
    name="test_agent",
    model="gemini-2.5-pro",
    instruction="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

app = App(
    name="test_app",
    root_agent=root_agent,
    context_cache_config=ContextCacheConfig(
        min_tokens=2048,
        ttl_seconds=1800,
        cache_intervals=10,
    ),
)

# Works:    adk web
# Broken:   adk web --session_service_uri=agentengine://<engine_id>
```

**How often has this issue occurred?:**
 - Always (100%)

title: [eslint-plugin-react-hooks] allow configuring custom hooks as "static"
body:
<!--
  Note: if the issue is about documentation or the website, please file it at:
  https://github.com/reactjs/reactjs.org/issues/new
-->

**Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?**

Feature/enhancement

**What is the current behavior?**

Currently the eslint plugin is unable to understand when the **return value** of a custom hook is static.

Example:

```jsx
import React from 'react'

function useToggle(init = false) {
  const [state, setState] = React.useState(init)
  const toggleState = React.useCallback(() => { setState(v => !v) }, [])
  return [state, toggleState]
}

function MyComponent({someProp}) {
  const [enabled, toggleEnabled] = useToggle()

  const handler = React.useCallback(() => {
    toggleEnabled()
    doSomethingWithTheProp(someProp)
  }, [someProp]) // exhaustive-deps warning for toggleEnabled

  return <button onClick={handler}>Do something</button>
}
```

**What is the expected behavior?**

I would like to configure `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` to tell it that `toggleEnabled` is static and doesn't need to be included in a dependency array. This isn't a huge deal but more of an ergonomic papercut that discourages writing/using custom hooks.

As for how/where to configure it, I would be happy to add something like this to my .eslintrc:

```js
{
  "staticHooks": {
    "useToggle": [false, true],  // first return value is not stable, second is
    "useForm": true,             // entire return value is stable 
  }
}
```

Then the plugin could have an additional check [after these 2 checks](https://github.com/facebook/react/blob/8b580a89d6dbbde8a3ed69475899addef1751116/packages/eslint-plugin-react-hooks/src/ExhaustiveDeps.js#L228-L231) that tests for custom names.

**Which versions of React, and which browser / OS are affected by this issue? Did this work in previous versions of React?**

All versions of eslint-plugin-react-hooks have the same deficiency.

## Please read my first comment below and try my fork if you are interested in this feature!

title: Feature request: provide optional install groups for hydrated datastore dependencies
body:
We've observed flakiness at step transition due to pypi being unreachable. Since the required datastore packages don't change in our configuration, we've opted to set `METAFLOW_SKIP_INSTALL_DEPENDENCIES` and manually install the set of required packages in our base image. 

This seems drift prone if the required set of packages changes: since the list for each datastore type is known in advance in `_get_install_dependencies_cmd`, could these be exposed as extras groups in setup.py for easier pre-baking?

title: Codex Desktop: automatic context compaction cannot be cancelled and can stall long-running tasks
body:
## Environment
- Product: Codex Desktop App
- OS: Windows
- App version: 26.519.5221.0
- Date observed: 2026-05-25
- Model: gpt-5.5

## Issue
During a long-running analysis session, Codex automatically started compacting the conversation context. The UI remained at `正在自动压缩上下文` ("Automatically compacting context") for more than 1 minute 54 seconds.

There is no visible way to cancel the operation. Sending a message asking Codex to stop compacting does not interrupt it.

## Impact
This makes long-running analytical and coding tasks unreliable. Automatic compaction can lose important reasoning context and, when it stalls, prevents continuing the task in the active conversation.

## Expected behavior
- Provide an option to disable automatic compaction or make it manual.
- Provide a Cancel button for an in-progress compaction.
- Show meaningful progress or recovery when compaction stalls.
- Preserve task-critical state across compactions.

A screenshot is available showing the UI stuck at `正在自动压缩上下文` after `1m 54s`.

title: Tracking Issue for LoongArch CRC Intrinsics
body:
This is a tracking issue for the CRC intrinsics on LoongArch64.
No feature gate is required for this issue, since the CRC instructions are part of the base LoongArch64 v1.0 ISA.
CRC instructions are only defined for the LoongArch64 ISA and are not available on LoongArch32.

### Public API

The `stdarch_loongarch_crc` feature exposes 8 functions, note that these do not require a target feature to call:

```rust
/// Calculate the CRC value using the IEEE 802.3 polynomial (0xEDB88320)
pub fn crc_w_b_w(a: i8, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the IEEE 802.3 polynomial (0xEDB88320)
pub fn crc_w_h_w(a: i16, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the IEEE 802.3 polynomial (0xEDB88320)
pub fn crc_w_w_w(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the IEEE 802.3 polynomial (0xEDB88320)
pub fn crc_w_d_w(a: i64, b: i32) -> i32 

/// Calculate the CRC value using the Castagnoli polynomial (0x82F63B78)
pub fn crcc_w_b_w(a: i8, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the Castagnoli polynomial (0x82F63B78)
pub fn crcc_w_h_w(a: i16, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the Castagnoli polynomial (0x82F63B78)
pub fn crcc_w_w_w(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32

/// Calculate the CRC value using the Castagnoli polynomial (0x82F63B78)
pub fn crcc_w_d_w(a: i64, b: i32) -> i32
```

### Steps
<!--
Include each step required to complete the feature. Typically this is a PR
implementing a feature, followed by a PR that stabilises the feature. However
for larger features an implementation could be broken up into multiple PRs.
-->

- [ ] Implement the RFC (cc @rust-lang/XXX -- can anyone write up mentoring
      instructions?)
- [ ] Adjust documentation ([see instructions on rustc-dev-guide][doc-guide])
- [ ] Style updates for any new syntax ([nightly-style-procedure])
  - [ ] Style team decision on new formatting
  - [ ] Formatting for new syntax has been added to the [Style Guide]
  - [ ] (non-blocking) Formatting has been implemented in `rustfmt`
- [ ] Stabilization PR ([see instructions on rustc-dev-guide][stabilization-guide])

[stabilization-guide]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/stabilization_guide.html#stabilization-pr
[doc-guide]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/stabilization_guide.html#documentation-prs
[nightly-style-procedure]: https://github.com/rust-lang/style-team/blob/main/nightly-style-procedure.md 
[Style Guide]: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/tree/HEAD/src/doc/style-guide

### Unresolved Questions
<!--
Include any open questions that need to be answered before the feature can be
stabilised. It is useful to link related discussions and conculsions as they
develop.
-->

- [ ] list all the "unresolved questions" found in the RFC to ensure they are
  not forgotten.

### Implementation history

- https://github.com/rust-lang/stdarch/pull/1688
- https://github.com/rust-lang/stdarch/pull/2136

title: Prune not respecting .gitignore
body:
### Verify canary release

- [x] I verified that the issue exists in the latest Turborepo canary release.

### Link to code that reproduces this issue

https://github.com/tct-willr/turborepo-gitignore

### Which canary version will you have in your reproduction?

2.9.15-canary.8

### Environment information

```block
• turbo 2.9.15-canary.8
CLI:
   Version: 2.9.15-canary.8
   Path to executable: <REDACTED>/turborepo-gitignore/node_modules/.pnpm/@turbo+darwin-arm64@2.9.15-canary.8/node_modules/@turbo/darwin-arm64/bin/turbo
   Daemon status: Not running
   Package manager: pnpm

Platform:
   Architecture: aarch64
   Operating system: macos
   WSL: false
   Available memory (MB): 9700
   Available CPU cores: 12

Environment:
   CI: None
   AI agent: None
   Terminal (TERM): xterm-kitty
   Terminal program (TERM_PROGRAM): unknown
   Terminal program version (TERM_PROGRAM_VERSION): unknown
   Shell (SHELL): /bin/zsh
   stdin: false
   Node.js version: v24.15.0
```

### Expected behavior

I expect `.gitignore` to be respected across all apps/packages when I run `turbo prune app-a --docker`.

### Actual behavior

Root-level `.gitignore` is not being respected when running `pnpm turbo prune app-a --docker`. Thus, `node_modules` are being copied into eg. `out/full/packages/pkg-a/node_modules`.

### To Reproduce

1. Run `pnpm turbo prune app-a --docker`
2. Run `ls out/full/packages/pkg-a`

```bash 
➜ ls out/full/packages/pkg-a     
node_modules package.json README.md
```

To reproduce expected behaviour;
1. `pnpm install turbo@2.9.6 -w`
2. `rm -rf ./out`
3. `pnpm turbo prune app-a --docker`
4. `ls out/full/packages/pkg-a`

```bash
➜ ls out/full/packages/pkg-a   
package.json README.md
```

### Additional context

This appears to have been introduced between `2.9.6` and `2.9.7`. I suspect it might be #12662 but I haven't reviewed the full Rust code to understand the broader implications.

I noticed this while updating `turborepo` in my companies monorepo when our Dockerfile build failed due to symlinks existing in the packages/node_modules.

title: Extend the Goal drivers section in Site Goals widgets
body:
## Feature Description

This issue will continue building on the "Goal drivers" section implemented in #12528  and also implement remaining 4 of the 7 metric tiles for these drivers, and allow showing up to 6 drivers up to 2 rows . [Design doc section](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1n)

---------------

_Do not alter or remove anything below. The following sections will be managed by moderators only._

## Acceptance criteria

* The Goal Drivers section should support rendering up to 6 driver tiles in each of the Site Goals widgets, laid out as max 2 rows x 3 columns.
* This issue adds the remaining 4 Goal Drivers (excluding the 3 already delivered in #12528) matching [(Figma mocks for individual tiles)](https://www.figma.com/design/S2CNc1toIzC7UrGMgs79S6/Site-Goals?node-id=5551-85368&m=dev):
  * Online store: "Sales by cities", "Sales by countries", "Top authors driving sales", "Sales by device type".
  * Lead generation: "Leads by cities", "Leads by countries", "Top authors driving leads", "Leads by device type".
* When less then 6 drivers are visible, in second row we should show empty slots after the selected drivers as per [figma example](https://www.figma.com/design/S2CNc1toIzC7UrGMgs79S6/Site-Goals?node-id=5151-55252&t=zCHr6J2Tgnezugy5-0)

## Implementation Brief

#### Goal drivers section layout extension

* Extend `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/GoalDriversSection.tsx` to support rendering up to 6 active driver tiles in a fixed 2-row x 3-column layout.
  * Reuse existing section logic/components; only change layout/rendering behavior for driver count > 3.
  * Render active drivers first (in resolver order), then render empty slot cells to fill the grid to 6 cells total.
  * Placeholder count formula: `emptySlots = Math.max( 0, 6 - activeDrivers.length )`.
  * This ensures when 4/5 drivers are visible, row 2 shows trailing empty slots; when 6 are visible, no empty slots are rendered.

* Add explicit empty-slot rendering in `GoalDriversSection` for row-2 gaps.
  * Add a small presentational `GoalDriverEmptySlot` (or inline markup) and append it after active drivers.
  * Empty slots are visual-only in this issue (non-interactive), matching Figma spacing and card dimensions.

* Update Goal Drivers section styles for 3-column grid wrapping.
  * Add a dedicated stylesheet under `assets/sass/components/analytics-4/site-goals/` (for example `_googlesitekit-goal-drivers.scss`) and import to the `assets/sass/components/analytics-4/_index.scss`.
  * Mobile keeps 1-column stacking; tablet/desktop uses 3 equal columns with consistent gaps so row 2 is visually aligned.

#### Goal drivers catalog extension (remaining 4)

* Extend existing `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/registry.ts` with 4 new driver IDs: `cities`, `countries`, `topAuthors`, `deviceType`.
  * Each new entry should include existing metadata structure (`id`, `title`, `order`, `defaultEnabled`, `Component`) and goal-specific copy mapping (sales/lead labels).

#### New goal driver hooks

* Keep hook contract identical to existing hooks from #12528.

* Add `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/hooks/useCitiesGoalDriverData.ts`.
  * query by `city` + `eventCount`, apply event filtering, aggregate by city key, sort desc, normalize rows.

* Add `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/hooks/useCountriesGoalDriverData.ts`.
  * query by `country` + `eventCount`, apply event filtering, aggregate by country key, sort desc, normalize rows.

* Add `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/hooks/useTopAuthorsGoalDriverData.ts`.
  * follow existing GA4 author/custom-dimension pattern used elsewhere in the codebase, then event-filter, aggregate, sort, normalize.

* Add `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/hooks/useDeviceTypeGoalDriverData.ts`.
  * query by device category + `eventCount`, apply event filtering, aggregate by device key, sort desc, normalize rows.

* Extend existing `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/hooks/useGoalDriversData.ts` to include the 4 new hook outputs.
  * Keep explicit hook calls (fixed order), merge via `dataByID`, then resolve visible drivers through existing active-ID resolver.
  * Return drivers capped to 6 and preserve resolved order so layout is deterministic.

#### Goal driver tile components

* Add presentational tiles in `assets/js/modules/analytics-4/components/site-goals/goal-drivers/`: `CitiesGoalDriver.tsx`, `CountriesGoalDriver.tsx`, `TopAuthorsGoalDriver.tsx`, `DeviceTypeGoalDriver.tsx`.
  * Follow same `MetricTileTable` pattern as existing 3 GoalDrivers; accept only normalized props (`rows`, `loading`, `error`, `limit`).

* Keep `OnlineStorePerformanceWidget` and `LeadGenerationPerformanceWidget` integration pattern from #12528 and only extend selected driver inputs to allow up to 6 visible IDs.
  * No new report logic in widget containers; all new data logic remains in hooks.

### Test Coverage

* Add tests for 6-cell layout behavior in `GoalDriversSection`.
  * Verify 4 drivers => 2 empty slots; 5 drivers => 1 empty slot; 6 drivers => 0 empty slots.
  * Verify driver cards are rendered before placeholders and order is preserved.

* Add tests for registry/resolver updates.
  * Verify new IDs are recognized, defaults still return existing top 3, and selected combinations can include new drivers up to max 6.

* Add tests for the 4 new hooks.
  * Verify ecommerce primary-event filtering, lead multi-event aggregation, normalized output shape, sorting, and fallback labels where relevant.

* Add integration tests for both widgets with mixed 6-driver selections.
  * Confirm new drivers render correctly for ecommerce and lead variants and that empty slots appear when selected count is 4 or 5.

* Add/update Stories to show second row / 4-6 drivers with and without empty slots showing.

## QA Brief

* Setup Site Kit with `siteGoals` feature flag enabled
  * You can connect wexford website it has ecomm data for 90 days period
* Verify that when `Select metrics` in Site goals widget is clicked it opens side panel with 7 goal drivers available (3 are selected by default)
* Verify that changing the selection and saving it is reflected in site goals widgets
* Verify that you can select up to 6 and minimum 1 (error notice is shown on selecting 0 or 7 and saving should be disabled)
* Verify that when up to 3 goal drivers are selected only one row is shown, and selecting 4 or more is showing 2 rows 
* Verify it works for view only users
* Available goal drivers should have title and description matching the [metrics list doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/12sQb751gDHrCch8HlFV5cI6Qa-ZD_3XHEd08e-Mf-vA/edit?tab=t.0)

Note to QA:
- To QA and validate the issues raised under https://github.com/google/site-kit-wp/issues/12578#issuecomment-4501969849 to ensure these are fixed.
- Top Authors goal driver will be added in #12734

title: `pext/yotta:latest` image type has been deprecated and stopped working with latest docker
body:
Trying to build Makecode with Docker v26+ fails, as trying to pull the `pext/yotta:latest` image (for the V2 CODAL build) throws the following error:

```
$ docker pull pext/yotta:latest
latest: Pulling from pext/yotta
[DEPRECATION NOTICE] Docker Image Format v1 and Docker Image manifest version 2, schema 1 support is disabled by default and will be removed in an upcoming release. Suggest the author of docker.io/pext/yotta:latest to upgrade the image to the OCI Format or Docker Image manifest v2, schema 2. More information at https://docs.docker.com/go/deprecated-image-specs/
```
https://github.com/microsoft/pxt-microbit/blob/bd4bda19f33ba30ea61e3620c8fa069b8306d07e/pxtarget.json#L204-L207

This docker image type was deprecated in Docker v19, it has been disabled already in v26, and v27 (which was release a couple of weeks ago) has completely and permanently removed the functionality:
https://docs.docker.com/engine/deprecated/#pushing-and-pulling-with-image-manifest-v2-schema-1

The GH Actions runner images (which we use in the CODAL CI to test changes with a MakeCode build) currently use Docker v26, which needs a workaround to enable this image type, but it won't be long before they upgrade to v27 and things will stop working if there isn't a way to downgrade the docker version from the images.

title: Context window usage indicator is not displayed
body: ### What version of the IDE extension are you using? 26.519.32039 ### What subscription do you have? Plus ### Which IDE are you using? VS Code ### What platform is your computer? Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0 x64 ### What issue are you seeing? Just noticed that the context window usage indicator is no longer displayed. Before: Now: ### What steps can reproduce the bug? 1. Use an earlier version of the Codex VS Code extension. 2. Use the current version of the Codex VS Code extension. 3. Notice that the context window usage indicator is no longer displayed. ### What is the expected behavior? The context window usage indicator should be displayed. ### Additional information Without this indicator, it is impossible to understand the current context window usage. This information is essential for users who have long chat sessions, because when the context becomes large enough, it is automatically compacted. Since we know that not all the information is preserved during compaction, it is important to know when the context is going to be compacted at the beginning of the next turn. This critical part of the current state should be visible without any additional actions. It is as important as the battery indicator on a phone and should remain visible in all cases.

title: `Allocator::grow` documentation
body:
### Location (URL)

https://doc.rust-lang.org/core/alloc/trait.Allocator.html#method.grow

### Summary

The current documentation for `Allocator::grow` has parts which seem a little ambiguous, as discussed on Zulip [here](https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/197181-t-libs.2Fwg-allocators/topic/Documentation.20for.20.60Allocator.3A.3Agrow.60).

In particular, the current phrasing talks about memory ownership being "transferred to this allocator," which a user could misinterpret as referring to memory transfer between two allocators, instead of from the caller back to the original allocator.

Additionally, it seems like "memory/memory block" are used to refer to both an allocation with full temporal provenance (when talking about ownership transfer back to the allocator) and the memory contents without temporal provenance attached (when talking about how the old memory contents are accessible through the newly returned pointer, which comes with new temporal provenance).

Putting these together, I propose to change the documentation to look like this, with changes in **bold**. Here I replaced "returned" with "transferred" and wrote "memory block" instead of "allocation," to clarify the two points above. I also added another clarifying sentence, as per a suggestion in the Zulip conversation.
> If this returns `Ok`, then ownership of the **allocation** referenced by `ptr` has been **returned** to this allocator. Any access to the old `ptr` is Undefined Behavior, even if the allocation was grown in-place. **In particular, it is UB to offset the old `ptr`, except if the offset is 0, since it is now derived from a freed allocation.** The newly returned pointer is the only valid pointer for accessing this memory now.
>
> If this method returns `Err`, then ownership of the **allocation** has not been **returned** to this allocator, and the contents of the memory block are unaltered.

title: Android Remote Control does not load host thread history (no thread/list requests observed)
body:
### Summary

When using **ChatGPT Android → Remote Control → connect to a Mac running Codex Desktop**, the mobile UI does not show any existing host conversation history (local/persisted threads). The host appears to receive the connection, but **the Android remote client never issues `thread/list` (or `thread/loaded/list`) to the host app-server**, so there is nothing to render.

As a result, Remote Control on mobile looks like it has “no remote threads loaded” / cannot load history.

### Environment

- **Host**: macOS 26.3 (Build 25D125), Darwin 25.3.0 arm64
- **Codex Desktop**: 26.513.20950 (CFBundleVersion 2816)
- **Bundled CLI**: `codex-cli 0.131.0-alpha.9`
- **Codex home**: `~/.codex`

### What I expected

After Android Remote Control connects to the host, the mobile UI should be able to display host thread history by calling:

- `thread/list` (persisted history) and/or
- `thread/loaded/list` (in-memory loaded threads)

### What actually happens

From host-side logs (`~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite`), Android remote connections only call:

- `initialize`
- `thread/start`

No `thread/list`, `thread/loaded/list`, `thread/read`, or `thread/resume` is observed from the Android remote client, so persisted threads never appear on mobile.

### Evidence (host logs)

I queried `~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite` for the Android remote client name `codex_chatgpt_android_remote`.

Observed methods (deduped):

- `rpc.method="initialize"` / `client_name="codex_chatgpt_android_remote"`
- `rpc.method="thread/start"` / `client_name="codex_chatgpt_android_remote"`

No entries for:

- `rpc.method="thread/list"`
- `rpc.method="thread/loaded/list"`

Example log line (redacted/truncated):

```
app_server.request{... rpc.method="thread/start" ... app_server.client_name="codex_chatgpt_android_remote" ...}
```

### Additional notes

- The host has many local threads in `~/.codex/state_5.sqlite` and `thread/list` works when called locally.

### Repro steps

1. On Mac, open Codex Desktop and ensure Remote Control is enabled.
2. On Android ChatGPT app, open Remote Control and connect to the Mac host.
3. Observe that mobile Remote Control does not show host history.
4. Check host logs (`~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite`): only `initialize` + `thread/start` from `codex_chatgpt_android_remote`.

### Suspected cause

Mobile Remote Control UI may not be issuing `thread/list` at all (or the request is being filtered client-side by `sourceKinds` such that it never reaches the host).

title: Security: OWASP ASI06 memory poisoning defense for Vercel AI SDK agent memory
body:
## Memory Poisoning Defense for Vercel AI SDK Agents (OWASP ASI06)

Hi Vercel AI SDK team 👋

The Vercel AI SDK is widely used to build production AI applications with tool use and memory. As these applications grow more agentic, **ASI06 — Memory Poisoning** (from the [OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2025](https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/)) is an emerging threat.

### The Threat

Malicious content written into an agent's memory (message history, tool call results) can persist and silently alter future model behavior — bypassing all model-level safety measures.

### Proposed Integration

[**OWASP Agent Memory Guard**](https://github.com/OWASP/www-project-agent-memory-guard) is an official OWASP reference implementation for detecting and blocking memory poisoning attempts before they are written to any store.

```typescript
import { MemoryGuard } from 'agent-memory-guard'; // Python package, callable via subprocess or API

// Before appending to messages array
const result = await guard.scan(newMessage.content);
if (result.isSafe) {
  messages.push(newMessage);
}
```

**Python package:** `pip install agent-memory-guard`

### Ask

Would the Vercel AI SDK team be open to:
1. A security note in the `useChat` / `streamText` memory management docs about ASI06?
2. Or a TypeScript-compatible interface for memory validation hooks?

Happy to draft a docs PR or prototype a JS/TS wrapper.

title: @batch step fails locally with 'No IAM role specified' when no per-job IAM role is configured
body:
On EC2 compute environments that use an instance profile for container credentials, running any `@batch` step fails immediately — before the job reaches AWS — with:

```
BatchJobException: Unable to launch AWS Batch job. No IAM role specified.
```

`METAFLOW_ECS_S3_ACCESS_IAM_ROLE` is not set; the container is expected to acquire credentials from the EC2 instance profile.

**Metaflow version:** master, `4871fb1`
**Deployment:** AWS Batch on EC2 compute environment, no per-job IAM role

**Steps to reproduce**
1. EC2 compute environment using instance-profile credentials
2. `METAFLOW_ECS_S3_ACCESS_IAM_ROLE` not set
3. Run any `@batch`-decorated flow

**Expected**
Job submits successfully; the [AWS Batch `ContainerProperties` API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/batch/latest/APIReference/API_ContainerProperties.html) documents `jobRoleArn` as optional.

**Actual**
The guard in [`batch_client.py` (L82–85)](https://github.com/Netflix/metaflow/blob/4871fb1e7d33de5cfb63f64c57ebe33304fd3376/metaflow/plugins/aws/batch/batch_client.py#L82-L85) raises unconditionally when `iam_role is None`, and [`_register_job_definition`](https://github.com/Netflix/metaflow/blob/4871fb1e7d33de5cfb63f64c57ebe33304fd3376/metaflow/plugins/aws/batch/batch_client.py#L191) always passes `"jobRoleArn": job_role` to boto3 even when `job_role` is `None`, which boto3 rejects for string fields.

title: `semver_ord` produces non-semver ordering on databases with a non-`C` collation
body:
### Current Behavior

On a database with a non-`C` collation, `semver_ord` orders prerelease identifiers wrong:
```
1.0.0-B > 1.0.0-alpha
```

Per spec:
> Identifiers with letters or hyphens are compared lexically in ASCII sort order.

`B` (0x42) sorts before `a` (0x61), so `1.0.0-B` should come before `1.0.0-alpha`, not after.

The official `postgres` image defaults to `en_US.utf8` (`docker run --rm postgres:16 printenv LANG`), so the devcontainer hits it too. `calculate_default_version` orders by `semver_ord`, so `default_version` can be wrong on any non-`C` deployment.

### Expected Behavior

`semver_ord` orders by ASCII regardless of the database collation:
```
1.0.0-alpha > 1.0.0-B
```

### Steps To Reproduce

Add an uppercase test case. `test_spec_order` only uses lowercase identifiers today, which sort the same under `C` and `en_US`, so it does not catch this:

```diff
diff --git a/crates/crates_io_database/tests/semver_ord.rs b/crates/crates_io_database/tests/semver_ord.rs
index 0a5dbadc1..607685a15 100644
--- a/crates/crates_io_database/tests/semver_ord.rs
+++ b/crates/crates_io_database/tests/semver_ord.rs
@@ -75,7 +75,8 @@ async fn test_spec_order() {
                     '1.0.0-beta.2',
                     '1.0.0-alpha.1',
                     '1.0.0-alpha.beta',
-                    '1.0.0-beta.11'
+                    '1.0.0-beta.11',
+                    '1.0.0-B'
                 ]) as num
             )
             select num
@@ -92,6 +93,7 @@ async fn test_spec_order() {
 
     insta::assert_debug_snapshot!(check("asc").await, @r"
     [
+        1.0.0-B,
         1.0.0-alpha,
         1.0.0-alpha.1,
         1.0.0-alpha.beta,
@@ -113,6 +115,7 @@ async fn test_spec_order() {
         1.0.0-alpha.beta,
         1.0.0-alpha.1,
         1.0.0-alpha,
+        1.0.0-B,
     ]
     ");
 }
```

```bash
export TEST_DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres@localhost:55432/test"
```

Failing:
```bash
docker run --rm -e POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD=trust -e POSTGRES_DB=test -p 55432:5432 postgres:17
cargo test -p crates_io_database --test semver_ord test_spec_order
```

Works only with explicit `C`-collation:
```bash
docker run --rm -e POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD=trust -e POSTGRES_DB=test -e POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS=--lc-collate=C -p 55432:5432 postgres:17
cargo test -p crates_io_database --test semver_ord test_spec_order
```

### Environment

n/a

### Anything else?

* The devcontainer should set up postgres with explicit `C` collation (`POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS=--lc-collate=C`).
* `test_spec_order` should include an uppercase vs lowercase case.
* Ideally `semver_ord` should be collation independent.
* If `C` stays a requirement, `semver_ord` should document it.

title: `useTransition` stuck in loading state when using `router.refresh()` after calling a server action
body:
### Link to the code that reproduces this issue

https://github.com/vinassefranche/use-transition-hanging

### To Reproduce

1. build the application as the issue only occurs on the built version: `pnpm run build`
2. serve the application: `pnpm run start`
3. go to http://localhost:3000/ in a browser
4. click on the "trigger action" button
5. see the button being stuck in `loading...` state and the render date not being updated

If the button is not stuck, click on it a few times. If still not, re-start the application and try again. If not, add more `<BigBlock />` in the page as it seems like the more blocks we add, the more likely the issue is to occur

### Current vs. Expected behavior

The page is not re-rendered and the button is stuck on the loading state while the page should be re-rendered and the button not in loading state.
The issue is not always present though, even though it's there most of the case. It can occur on the first click but can work a few times and then not work. It seems like the more content I add to the page, the more likely the issue is to occur.

Here's a video of the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ee33bef-86a0-4282-8205-1458f1748e54

Here are a few things that I found made the issue disappear:
- remove the `loading.tsx `file
- downgrade `next` to the version 15.4.7 (works even when specifying --turbopack to the build command)
- keep only one `<BigBlock />` in the page
- stop awaiting `fakeDataRetrieval()`

### Provide environment information

```bash

  Platform: linux
  Arch: x64
  Version: #29~24.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Jun 26 14:16:59 UTC 2
  Available memory (MB): 63932

title: aws_batch_tags / METAFLOW_BATCH_DEFAULT_TAGS silently ignored unless METAFLOW_BATCH_EMIT_TAGS=true
body:
Tags set via `@batch(aws_batch_tags={"CostCenter": "123"})` or the `METAFLOW_BATCH_DEFAULT_TAGS` environment variable are silently dropped when `METAFLOW_BATCH_EMIT_TAGS` is not set. No warning or error is raised.

In enterprise deployments this is more than cosmetic: many organizations enforce **mandatory tagging policies** via AWS Service Control Policies (SCPs) or AWS Config rules. When required tags (e.g. `CostCenter`, `Team`, `Environment`) are absent, AWS rejects the resource operation entirely, causing the Batch job submission to fail with an access-denied error that gives no hint the root cause is a Metaflow configuration flag.

**Metaflow version:** master, `4871fb1`
**Deployment:** AWS Batch
**`METAFLOW_BATCH_EMIT_TAGS`:** not set (default `False`)

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Set `@batch(aws_batch_tags={"CostCenter": "123"})` (or `METAFLOW_BATCH_DEFAULT_TAGS='{"CostCenter": "123"}'`)
2. Do not set `METAFLOW_BATCH_EMIT_TAGS`
3. Run the flow

**Without a mandatory tagging SCP:** job submits but has no tags — silent data loss.
**With a mandatory tagging SCP:** job submission is rejected by AWS with an access-denied or tag-policy-violation error, making the flow completely unusable even though the user has correctly configured the required tags.

**Expected**
Tags explicitly provided by the user are applied to the job regardless of `METAFLOW_BATCH_EMIT_TAGS`. That flag is meant to control Metaflow's own internal observability tags (`app=metaflow`, `metaflow.flow_name`, etc.), not whether operator-defined cost-attribution or compliance tags take effect.

**Actual**
The `aws_batch_tags` block sits inside the [`if BATCH_EMIT_TAGS:` guard in `batch.py` (L380–L403)](https://github.com/Netflix/metaflow/blob/4871fb1e7d33de5cfb63f64c57ebe33304fd3376/metaflow/plugins/aws/batch/batch.py#L380-L403) (introduced in `6c7766a`). User-defined tags share the guard with Metaflow's internal metadata tags even though their purpose — and the consequences of omitting them — are entirely different.

title: GPT-5.5 Fast suddenly feels as slow as Standard, with long thinking/context/search stalls
body:
### Summary
Over the past two days, GPT-5.5 Fast in Codex has felt significantly slower than before. It now feels closer to the usual Standard speed rather than Fast.

Some simple tasks that would normally complete quickly are now taking 10–20+ minutes, and sometimes even longer, before the task finishes. This feels like a clear regression compared with the previous normal behavior.

### What I am observing
- GPT-5.5 Fast feels noticeably slower than it did before.
- Simple tasks can take 10–20+ minutes or longer to complete.
- The “thinking” phase can get stuck for 40+ seconds, sometimes around a minute or more.
- Automatic context compression also appears to take a very long time.
- Reading and searching steps are also extremely slow.
- At the same time, GPT-5.5 Fast also seems less capable / less reliable than before, so the experience feels both slower and less effective.

### Why I do not think this is just my local machine or network
I do not believe this is caused by my computer performance. I am using a high-end MacBook Pro configuration:

- MacBook Pro 16-inch
- Apple M5 Max
- 128 GB memory
- macOS Tahoe 26.5
- Codex version 26.519.41501 (3044)

I also do not believe this is simply a network speed issue, because the slowdown is specifically noticeable in Codex/GPT-5.5 Fast behavior and is very different from how it normally performed before.

### Expected behavior
GPT-5.5 Fast should feel meaningfully faster than Standard, especially on simple tasks. Thinking, context compression, reading, and searching should not regularly stall for long periods on basic requests.

### Actual behavior
GPT-5.5 Fast currently feels like Standard speed or worse, with long stalls during thinking, automatic context compression, reading, and searching.

### Request
Could the team please check whether there has been a recent performance regression, routing change, capacity issue, or other backend/client issue affecting GPT-5.5 Fast in Codex?

I am also curious whether other users are seeing the same thing, because this slowdown feels very obvious compared with the previous normal GPT-5.5 Fast experience.

title: Organization display names are not respected
body:
### Current Behavior

On GitHub, organizations and users have two names.
Not sure what the correct terminology is, but one of them shows up in places like URLs and commits (for me that's 'netthier') and the other one, the display name, shows up in other places such as the profile or e-mails (which in my case is 'nett_hier' instead).
While my crates.io user page shows the former name, I'm listed with the latter name in any crates where I'm the owner:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66856670/222769572-f9fdec96-e4e5-4ed1-ac1c-98fa3a92fe36.png)
vs
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66856670/222769637-ad60c450-a6d8-4613-92f7-bfe97bba4c28.png)

However, for organizations, the former name is used in both cases, as can be seen in the above screenshot.


### Expected Behavior

As can be seen here, the display name of the organization is "SenseLabs", not "senselabsde": https://github.com/SenseLabsDE
My expectation would be that the organization owning the crate here is rendered as `SenseLabs` or `SenseLabs/crate-owners` instead (I'm not sure what the ideal way to include the team name here is) for consistency reasons.
Another option would be to allow making the display name of a team freely configurable.
Since the display name of a user on crates.io can be set to anything by simply changing it on GitHub and logging in again, the same should be possible for organizations.
A third option would be to at least respect the casing (i.e. "SenseLabsDE" instead of "senselabsde"), as that is what GitHub does in URLs as well.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create an organization with a display name different from the "proper" name
2. Create a team in said organization
3. Invite that team as an owner on a crate
4. Be saddened by the way the organization name is displayed

### Environment

(Not applicable)

### Anything else?

_No response_

On GitHub, organizations and users have two names.
Not sure what the correct terminology is, but one of them shows up in places like URLs and commits (for me that's 'netthier'

title: Use of Static Files in a Kit always returns `ERROR: inject kit: inject files: copy example.txt to container: copy to container: Error response from daemon: failed to read tar header: unexpected EOF`
body:
Static Files appear to never work. This is either a documentation issue where the documentation doesn't make it clear how to use Static Files or Static Files don't work.

# Steps to reproduce the problem

Create a Docker Sandbox Kit that looks like this

```
../sbx-kit-test/
├── files
│   └── home
│       └── test.txt
└── spec.yaml
```

With a `spec.yaml` that contains this

```yaml
schemaVersion: "1"
kind: mixin
name: sbx-test
displayName: sbx-test
description: A test which just contains a single file
```

Next create a Docker Sandbox

```
sbx create --name claude-sbx-test claude .
```

And then try to add the kit to the sandbox with the command

```
sbx kit add claude-sbx-test ../sbx-kit-test/
```

# Expected results

The single `test.txt` file should be added to the sandbox at `/home/agent/test.txt`

# Actual results

```
sbx kit add claude-sbx-test ..//sbx-kit-test/
Adding kit 'sbx-test' to sandbox 'claude-sbx-test'...
  📦 Copying 1 file(s)...
    -> /home/agent/test.txt (mode 0664)
ERROR: inject kit: inject files: copy test.txt to container: copy to container: Error response from daemon: failed to read tar header: unexpected EOF
```

# Version

```
# sbx version
Client Version:  v0.28.3 8d114184e520cf5744502f187faf90342af599e5
Server Version:  v0.28.3 8d114184e520cf5744502f187faf90342af599e5
```

title: Make `krel fast-forward` work for other repositories like k/website
body:
There is a sync job in place which fast-forwards the kubernetes/kubernetes `release-1.x` branch from `master` until the final (non-prerelease) tag exists on it. The job runs periodically in prow and does basically nothing when we're not in code freeze: https://testgrid.k8s.io/sig-release-releng-blocking#git-repo-kubernetes-fast-forward

The idea is to re-use the logic for other repositories like `k/website` to sync the documentation development branch during the release cycle. We have to make the tool more flexible from a CLI perspective and double check the code paths for possible adaptations.

The entrypoint for the code of `krel fast-forward` is there: https://github.com/kubernetes/release/blob/master/cmd/krel/cmd/ff.go

title: CI: Improvements for the cards component
body:
Managing the nested npm project for the cards component could benefit from additional CI. Right now we only have verification in place that the project can be built successfully, which was required to have some level of trust for Dependabot package upgrade suggestions.

Some ideas for possible improvements

## Auditing packages
Dependabot is the main source for package version upgrades, but it should be considered whether these should be blindly trusted. At minimum, we could add `npm audit` as part of the PR checks when the changes touch the cards component

## Build&Commit card changes
When changing any code in the Cards UI, or upgrading packages, this will not have any effect for the component itself unless the project is also built. Currently there is no CI present, instead the author needs to manually rebuild the assets as part of their PR.

This could be automated as well, with a step taking care or rebuilding the assets and adding a commit to the PR. Some security considerations might apply when implementing commit rights for an action.

title: Cloud chat converted locally attaches to active local project instead of original project
body:
## Summary

When converting or continuing a cloud Codex chat locally from an existing project, Codex appears to attach the chat/session to the currently active local project rather than the original project associated with the cloud chat.

This only behaves correctly when the original project is the only currently active local project.

## Expected behavior

A cloud chat that originated from Project A should convert/continue locally in Project A, regardless of which other local project is currently active.

If Codex cannot safely determine the target project, it should prompt the user to confirm the local project/workspace before attaching the chat.

## Actual behavior

When another local project is active, the cloud chat is forcibly brought into that active local project, even when that project is different from the original project.

This can cause confusion and may risk applying edits or context to the wrong local workspace.

## Reproduction steps

1. Have at least two local Codex projects available, Project A and Project B.
2. Start or have an existing cloud Codex chat associated with Project A.
3. Locally switch Codex so Project B is the active local project.
4. Convert or continue the cloud chat locally.
5. Observe that the chat/session attaches to Project B instead of Project A.

## Environment

- App: Codex Desktop
- OS: Windows
- Codex Desktop app package: `OpenAI.Codex_26.519.3891.0`
- Codex Desktop product version: `26.519.31651`
- Codex CLI / agent binary: `0.133.0-alpha.1`
- Git for Windows: `2.54.0.windows.1`
- Local workspace location includes Google Drive-backed folders

## Notes

This seems related to workspace/repo routing or stale active-project state. Similar existing reports include:

- #5057, where cloud task creation can target the wrong repo
- #10547, involving cloud/local state confusion
- #21076, involving stale or incorrect project/thread history state

The core concern is that Codex seems to prioritize the current active local project over the cloud chat's original project identity when converting/continuing the chat locally.

title: Stabilize `dyn Allocator`
body:
PR https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/156882 is stabilizing `Allocator`.

In order to do so more easily, it's marking `Allocator` as not `dyn`-compatible, to reserve the options of *either* adding features that currently prevent `dyn`-compatibility *or* marking it `dyn`-compatible and requiring those features to use an extension trait instead. 

In order to make this decision and the stabilization decision in parallel, I'm filing this issue for a separate FCP.

Based on the discussion in the All Hands, it seemed like there weren't any features that we *couldn't* do via the extension trait solution, allowing the base trait to be `dyn`-compatible. So, in this issue, I'm proposing that we remove the limitation on `Allocator` and allow `dyn Allocator`.

title: `sbx cp` fails with tar stream errors and leaves truncated files on Ubuntu 24.04 GCE VM
body:
## Summary

`sbx cp` fails when copying regular files from an Ubuntu 24.04 GCE host into a shell sandbox.

This is reproducible with synthetic random data and does not depend on any application repository, database, cloud storage bucket, or bootstrap script.

In one run, even a 1 MiB file failed with `tar: Unexpected EOF in archive` and left a truncated destination file inside the sandbox.

## Environment

- Google Compute Engine VM
- Machine type: `n2-standard-4`
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS amd64
- Kernel: `6.17.0-1013-gcp`
- Nested virtualization: enabled
- KVM modules loaded: `kvm_intel`, `kvm`
- Package: `docker-sbx 0.28.3-1~ubuntu.24.04~noble`
- `sbx` client/server: `v0.28.3 8d114184e520cf5744502f187faf90342af599e5`
- `sbx diagnose`: 7 passed

Example VM creation and ssh login command:

```bash
gcloud compute instances create sbx-cp-repro \
  --zone=asia-northeast1-a \
  --machine-type=n2-standard-4 \
  --image-family=ubuntu-2404-lts-amd64 \
  --image-project=ubuntu-os-cloud \
  --enable-nested-virtualization \
  --boot-disk-size=50GB

gcloud compute ssh sbx-cp-repro --zone=asia-northeast1-a
```

## Reproduction

```bash
# On a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 amd64 GCE VM with nested virtualization enabled:

curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sudo REPO_ONLY=1 sh
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y docker-sbx
sudo usermod -aG kvm "$USER"

# Start a new shell/session if needed so the group change applies.
sbx login

sbx version
sbx diagnose

mkdir -p "$HOME/sbx-cp-repro-mount"
sbx create --name sbx-cp-repro shell "$HOME/sbx-cp-repro-mount"
sbx ls

src=/tmp/sbx-cp-repro-1m.bin
dst=/tmp/sbx-cp-repro-1m.copy.bin

rm -f "$src"
sbx exec -u root sbx-cp-repro -- rm -f "$dst" || true

dd if=/dev/urandom of="$src" bs=1M count=1
sha256sum "$src"
wc -c "$src"

sbx cp "$src" "sbx-cp-repro:$dst"

sbx exec -u root sbx-cp-repro -- bash -lc "
  ls -lh '$dst' || true
  wc -c '$dst' || true
  sha256sum '$dst' || true
"
```

## Expected behavior

`sbx cp` should copy the file successfully.

The destination file should have the same size and SHA-256 hash as the source.

If the copy fails, it should not leave a truncated destination file behind.

## Actual behavior

For a 1 MiB file:

```text
ERROR: tar extract failed: tar: Unexpected EOF in archive
tar: Unexpected EOF in archive
tar: Error is not recoverable: exiting now
```

The destination file was left behind but truncated:

```text
source bytes:      1048576
destination bytes: 1048064
sha256 match:      no
```

Larger files also fail, sometimes with a different tar stream error. For example, a 64 MiB file failed with:

```text
Can't add file /tmp/sbx-cp-size-64m.bin to tar: io: read/write on closed pipe
Can't close tar writer: io: read/write on closed pipe
ERROR: write tar stream: write unix @->/home/<user>/.local/state/sandboxes/sandboxes/sandboxd/docker.sock: write: broken pipe
```

That run also left a truncated destination file:

```text
source bytes:      67108864
destination bytes: 52801536
sha256 match:      no
```

## Notes

The visible error varies between the receiver-side tar extraction error and the sender-side broken pipe error, but both appear to be the same class of failure: the `sbx cp` tar stream is interrupted and the sandbox can retain a partial destination file.

This is reproducible with synthetic random data and does not depend on any application reposi

title: Vulnerability discovered in Lemur
body:
Dear Lemur community,

In May 2026, an independent researcher disclosed a vulnerability identified in Lemur’s codebase. This vulnerability is related to an insecure default configuration.

Presently, we have no reason to believe that this vulnerability has been exploited.

We have already prepared the patch to fix this vulnerability, and will raise a PR to Lemur’s GitHub repository on the morning of Thursday, May 28 2026 PDT. On the same day, we will release a new version of Lemur (1.9.1) which will contain the patch. We recommend that all Lemur users upgrade immediately after version 1.9.1 has been released. We will disclose additional details regarding the vulnerability following the updated release of Lemur in the form of a GitHub security advisory.

Thanks,
Lemur Team
